attacking_style: Opportunistic attacker who strikes on final climbs with 3km to go when rivals show tactical misjudgment. Prefers small group sprints after prolonged breakaways rather than solo efforts.
team_tactics: Relies on domestiques for critical final-kilometer pacing (Vanhoucke-style) to set up small group sprints. Benefits when opponents have conflicting team interests that can be exploited.
terrain_preference: Multi-lap circuits with final climb finishing sequences and long breakaway stages (200km+) where tactical patience and positioning trump pure climbing power.
vulnerability: Struggles with effort distribution on ultra-long stages (227km Paris-Nice drop suggests pacing misjudgment). Vulnerable when teams execute coordinated chases without internal conflicts.
key_patterns: Wins from small breakaway groups (4 riders) via sprint finish, not solo attacks
Exploits rival team tactical confusion when multiple teammates present in different groups
Attacks final climb with 3km remaining, not long-range efforts
Maintains Grand Tour winning form across decade with tactical patience over raw power
Requires domestique pacing support in final 3-4km to set up winning sprint
form_trajectory: positive: 2022 Giro win suggests sustained elite tactical ability across career span, described as bookending decade of GT success
